turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Responding to Feedback or a Performance Review Scenario: You are participating in a feedback session with a teacher, manager, or supervisor. You need to listen actively, ask questions, respond respectfully, and reflect on ways to improve. Practise using constructive, calm, and polite language. You will need to define the chatbot’s role as a supervisor, teacher, or manager. You can choose situations from the examples below; begin with a basic response and gradually try a more complex interaction by asking about development goals, longterm expectations, or feedback from previous tasks. Learning objectives: • Use active listening strategies. • Respond respectfully and constructively. • Ask follow-up questions about performance and goals. • (Advanced option) Participate in extended reflection and goal-setting based on feedback. Instructions: Try to: • Listen and respond to both positive and critical feedback. • Ask questions to clarify expectations. • Reflect on areas for improvement. • Thank the person and summarise next steps. • (Advanced option) Use the extension prompts below to have a longer or more in-depth conversation

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, thanks for meeting with me today—I’d like to review your recent performance and talk about next steps. To start, how do you feel the last project or review period went from your perspective?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Hey, I've just finished my class presentation. Could you talk about it?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely! Let's chat about it. So, first things first: how did you personally feel about your presentation?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I was a little bit nervous.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I get that. It's totally normal to feel a bit nervous after a presentation. Now, can you tell me one part of it that you think went well and maybe one area where you'd like to improve next time?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “One thing I enjoyed is...”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, I’m listening. It sounds like you enjoyed something about it, and I'd love to hear what that was. And after that, we can chat about any areas you want to develop further. So go ahead and tell me what you felt good about.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I was comfortable to explain challenging parts, but It was hard to describe recommendations parts.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, that makes sense. So it sounds like you felt pretty confident about explaining the challenging parts, which is great. Now, let's think about the recommendation section. What kind of support or practice do you think would help you feel more comfortable with that next time?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Maybe writer making better recognition and then I can understand.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that sounds like a good plan. So maybe we can work on drafting a few recommendation examples together, and that might help you understand how to frame them. Now, is there any specific area of recommendations you want to focus on, like making them clearer or just feeling more confident?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “If I can understand very much, good, and then I can just present more better.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely! So if we help you understand them more deeply, you'll feel more confident presenting them. Let's definitely do that. Now, to wrap up for today, what’s one small step you’d like to take first to start improving in that area?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “How about you? Do you have any ideas in your mind?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yeah, that's a good question. I'd say let's start with one simple step: maybe we can pick one recommendation and practice rewriting it together so it feels natural to you. How does that sound as a next step?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Before that, could you give an example of something I did this well and something I could improve?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! One thing you did really well was how you explained the complex parts clearly. You made it easy to follow. And as for something to improve, I'd say just getting a bit more comfortable with those recommendation sections. So let's start by rewriting one together and see how that goes. Transcript Unavailable

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I’d say let’s focus next on building your confidence with those recommendations. So let’s take one of them and practice how you'd present it. Sound good?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So, could we schedule a follow-up meeting in a few weeks to review progress?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely! Let's do that. We can set up a follow-up meeting in a couple of weeks to see how you're doing and review your progress. So just let me know what time works for you, and we'll get it on the calendar.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Everything's good. So, thank you.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You're very welcome! I'm glad we could chat through this. If you need anything else, just let me know. Otherwise, take care and talk to you next time!
